Even after these numbers, he is dropped for the mighty Zimbabwe series. 😭🙏 https://t.co/VgUrGc6ndg

2076165680605610354.full_text:
Ravichandran Ashwin 🗣️ :

“England backed Jos Buttler despite a prolonged lean patch, and he repaid that faith with a strong comeback in this series. Meanwhile, India's T20 WC POTT Sanju Samson was given just three matches before being dropped. Then, in the final game, he's asked to prove himself all over again. Constantly changing the opening pair and never sticking with the same playing XI only creates more instability than clarity.”

2076164268744131005.full_text:
🚨AAKASH CHOPRA SLAMS TEAM MANEGEMENT ON DECISION TO PLAYING SANJU IN 50TH T20I 🚨

Aakash Chopra said 🗣️,

"Transition is difficult, but is the selection process right? What we did with Vaibhav Suryavanshi and Sanju Samson is a problem because we haven't benefited anyone. We gave Vaibhav Suryavanshi three consecutive chances for sure, he couldn't cross 15 runs in any of those and got out against short balls many times, but we gave only three chances.
You can't decide in three, whether it's a 15-year-old kid or a 25-year-old. When it's a dead rubber and you have already lost the series, you couldn't have gained anything from this. The only thing you could have gained was Vaibhav probably scoring runs. So you could have played Vaibhav here so that he could have scored runs and ended the England tour well."

2076149282378526867.full_text:
How Arrogant and bitter Gautam Gambhir was for a senior cricketer. On the job now, Each and every words of his comeback to bite now.👀🤡

Gautam Gambhir said🗣️,

"I am sure that Ravi Shastri doesn't know the records or hasn't watched the old series because he is a commentator. Often, it feels like when you haven't won anything yourself, you feel that the team you are coaching is the best team. Because you haven't won anything yourself, maybe the ODIs they won—the World Series they won—were the only ones they won. Other than that, I don't remember Ravi Shastri winning anything outside of India. Because outside of India, you won in New Zealand the last time, and that was after 40 years. I am sure that 40 years ago, Ravi Shastri wasn't playing for India. So, when you don't win yourself, you give such statements. And such statements—especially when you are the coach of the Indian team—should not be given, especially after losing 4-1 to England."

user.name: Cricket Central

2076138527499436348.full_text:
🚨 SHREYAS IYER SLAMS INDIA SELECTORS FOR PICKING SO MANY LEFT-HANDED BATTER IM SQUAD 🚨 

Shreyas Iyer said 🗣️,

"See, as I mentioned, that we needed to try the best combination that would be perfect on these conditions, and we also wanted a right-hander to go on with Abhishek. So that was one of the major reasons, and if you see, I was the only one out of all the left-handers who is a right-hander. So, as I mentioned earlier, we wanted to try a different combination, which would be suitable for this particular game, and he's a gun batsman; he has won so many series for us in the past. So, yeah, one of those reasons."
